articles.title: Building durable workflows on Postgres
articles.summary: Durable workflows are a simple but powerful tool for building reliable programs that can recover from crashes or failures. By regularly checkpointing program progress to a database, developers can reload from the last checkpoint to recover their work. Most commonly implemented via external orchestration, this pattern uses a central server to coordinate workflows and dispatch them to workers. However, an alternative approach is to use the database itself as an orchestrator, making it simpler and more efficient to implement durable workflows on Postgres.

articles.title: Package managers that package package managers
articles.summary: Mike Fiedler built a table to visualize how package managers interact with each other, showing that some packages are installed directly by the package manager, while others are packaged separately. The system package managers like apt and DNF have their own registries, which can be populated from outside sources such as ecosyste.ms and Repology. Conda sits between these two groups, carrying its own set of package managers alongside its compilers and runtimes. This setup allows for efficient distribution of packages across different platforms and languages.
